ryu-stand-light-punch.name: Standing Light Punch
notes: Can be rapid canceled

ryu-stand-medium-punch.name: Standing Medium Punch
ryu-stand-medium-punch.notes_sc: 2f extra recovery on whiff; useful combo tool with a Target Combo follow-up; extends a wider body hurtbox until the end of recovery

ryu-stand-heavy-punch.name: Standing Heavy Punch
notes: Forces the opponent into a standing position
ryu-stand-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Forces stand on hit (allowing Target Combo follow-up to work on crouching opponents); good poke with hitbox priority, many active frames, and a reactable hitconfirm window

ryu-stand-light-kick.name: Standing Light Kick
ryu-stand-light-kick.notes_sc: Good range for a 5f light (useful in punishes and neutral buffers); DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd active frame

ryu-stand-medium-kick.name: Standing Medium Kick
ryu-stand-medium-kick.notes_sc: Solid poke; front leg hurtbox is raised on frames 4-11 (can avoid low pokes)

ryu-stand-heavy-kick.name: Standing Heavy Kick
notes: Forces a juggle state when hitting a mid-air opponent
Produces a crumple stun for 36 frames when the attack lands as a Punish Counter
Juggle state time is extended when attack hits a mid-air opponent as a Punish Counter
ryu-stand-heavy-kick.notes_sc: Whiffs on crouching opponents unless they extend a vertical hurtbox; 2 extra recovery frames on block; good horizontal anti-air that puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state (higher launch on Punish Counter juggle); '''+11''' before Punish Counter crumple state goes airborne; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles

ryu-crouch-light-punch.name: Crouching Light Punch
notes: Can be rapid canceled
ryu-crouch-light-punch.notes_sc: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-5 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

ryu-crouch-medium-punch.name: Crouching Medium Punch
ryu-crouch-medium-punch.notes_sc: 2f extra recovery on whiff; combo and pressure tool; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-9 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air); extends a wider hurtbox until the end of recovery

ryu-crouch-heavy-punch.name: Crouching Heavy Punch
notes: Can only be canceled on the 1st active frame if the attack hits
Forces the opponent into a standing position
ryu-crouch-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Forces stand on hit; decent anti-air; cannot hit cross-ups; fist hurtbox is invincible to air strikes on frame 9; head hurtbox is invincible to air strikes on frames 10-14; only 1st active frame is cancelable (extends a combo-only hitbox to match the range of the 2nd active frame for combo consistency); () refers to hit/block recovery on 1st active frame only; 5f less hitstun on active frames 2-5 (can be punishable on meaty timing)

ryu-crouch-light-kick.name: Crouching Light Kick
notes: Can be rapid canceled

ryu-crouch-medium-kick.name: Crouching Medium Kick
ryu-crouch-medium-kick.notes_sc: Range: 1.482; DR cancel is delayed until after active frames

ryu-crouch-heavy-kick.name: Crouching Heavy Kick
notes: Knockdown time extended when attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter

ryu-jump-light-punch.name: Jumping Light Punch
ryu-jump-light-punch.notes_sc: Can be used as a fuzzy instant overhead

ryu-jump-medium-punch.name: Jumping Medium Punch
notes: Forces a juggle state when hitting a mid-air opponent
ryu-jump-medium-punch.notes_sc: Both hits are cancelable to Air Tatsu and put airborne opponents into a limited juggle state; if 1st hit is stand blocked, the 2nd hit will not hit overhead against crouch block; lower body projectile invuln 8-22f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

ryu-jump-heavy-punch.name: Jumping Heavy Punch
notes: Forces a slam knockdown on counter or Punish Counter when hitting a mid-air opponent
ryu-jump-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Spike knockdown on counter hit/Punish Counter vs. airborne opponents; lower body projectile invuln 9-23f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

ryu-jump-light-kick.name: Jumping Light Kick
notes: Has cross-up attack properties

ryu-jump-medium-kick.name: Jumping Medium Kick
notes: Has cross-up attack properties
ryu-jump-medium-kick.notes_sc: Cross-up; lower body projectile invuln 7-20f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

ryu-jump-heavy-kick.name: Jumping Heavy Kick
ryu-jump-heavy-kick.notes_sc: Strong jump-in from mid to long range; lower body projectile invuln 10-17f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

collarbone-breaker.name: Collarbone Breaker
notes: An overhead attack that cannot be blocked while crouching
Second attack becomes an overhead only if the first attack whiffs
collarbone-breaker.notes_sc: Counter-hit/Punish Counter/Drive Rush frame advantage applies to both hits; 2nd hit is no longer an overhead if the first hit connects; up to +5 on hit with meaty timing for a meterless combo extension

solar-plexus-strike.name: Solar Plexus Strike
notes: A forward-moving attack that allows you to act before an opponent even if blocked
solar-plexus-strike.notes_sc: 2 hits; good forward-moving combo starter and pressure tool; if 1st hit connects on an extended hurtbox, becomes '''+6''' on Counter-hit or '''+8''' on Punish Counter; Counter-hit/Punish Counter frame advantage applies to both hits; can be very advantageous if 1st hit trades with a light normal; 1st hit puts opponent into free juggle state so that the 2nd hit connects (to prevent Ryu from being punishable)

short-uppercut.name: Short Uppercut
notes: A quick strike that can be canceled into special moves
Can only be canceled on the 1st active frame if the attack hits
Forces the opponent into a standing position
short-uppercut.notes_sc: Forces stand on hit; only 1st active frame is cancelable; () refers to hit/block recovery on 1st active frame only; extremely short range makes it finicky in combos; 8f less hitstun on active frames 2-4 (can be punishable with meaty timing)

whirlwind-kick.name: Whirlwind Kick
notes: A kick with long reach. Useful as a counter to an opponent's sweeps
Can only be canceled with an Overdrive Aerial Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
whirlwind-kick.notes_sc: Cancelable to regular/OD Air Tatsu on hit/block; Low Crush on frames 13-20 (not airborne); high pushback on block; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; good juggle option after midscreen OD High Blade Kick for corner carry and oki; proximity guard box begins on frame 2 and reaches the maximum attack range (prevents opponents from walking back out of the attack startup)

axe-kick.name: Axe Kick
notes: A multi-hit kick that can be canceled into special moves
First attack Forces the opponent into a standing position
Second attack produces a ground bound on a mid-air hit
axe-kick.notes_sc: () refers to 2nd hit startup; 1st hit forces stand and puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state; 2nd hit puts airborne opponents into OTG bounce state; both hits are cancelable; Counter-hit and Punish Counter frame advantage applies to both hits

high-double-strike.name: High Double Strike
notes: A multi-hit attack with a lenient input window for follow-ups
Can only be canceled into Denjin Charge on hit.
high-double-strike.notes_sc: HK cannot hit vs. crouch block (much more unsafe); cancels to Denjin Charge by inputting 2P on hit only (KD Adv. +10); can juggle into MP/OD Shoryuken or SA3 in the corner

fuwa-triple-strike-2.name: Fuwa Triple Strike (2)
notes: A multi-hit attack that doesn't leave you wide open even if stopped partway through

fuwa-triple-strike-3.name: Fuwa Triple Strike (3)
notes: A multi-hit attack that doesn't leave you wide open even if stopped partway through
Can only be canceled into Denjin Charge on hit.

ryu-hadoken-light.name: Hadoken (Light)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range

ryu-hadoken-medium.name: Hadoken (Medium)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range

ryu-hadoken-heavy.name: Hadoken (Heavy)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range

ryu-hadoken-od.name: Hadoken (OD)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range

denjin-charge-hadoken.name: [Denjin Charge] Hadoken
notes: Envelop your fists with the Power of Nothingness, enhancing moves such as the Hadoken and Hashogeki. Leaves you wide open, making the timing of its usage critical

ryu-shoryuken-light.name: Shoryuken (Light)
notes: Leap into the air and perform a powerful uppercut. A Shoryuken is invincible to an opponent's jumping attacks, making it a great anti-air tool
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 14
Considered airborne from frames 7 - 34
Note: After the 3rd active frame this move deals only 800 damage

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 14

ryu-shoryuken-medium.name: Shoryuken (Medium)
notes: Leap into the air and perform a powerful uppercut. A Shoryuken is invincible to an opponent's jumping attacks, making it a great anti-air tool
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 9
Considered airborne from frames 8 - 44
Note: After the 3rd active frame this move deals only 800 damage

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 9

ryu-shoryuken-hp.name: Shoryuken (Heavy)
notes: Leap into the air and perform a powerful uppercut. A Shoryuken is invincible to an opponent's jumping attacks, making it a great anti-air tool
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 8
Considered airborne from frames 9 - 49
Note: After the 2nd active frame this move deals only 800 damage

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 8

ryu-shoryuken-od.name: Shoryuken (OD)
notes: Leap into the air and perform a powerful uppercut. A Shoryuken is invincible to an opponent's jumping attacks, making it a great anti-air tool
Completely invincible from frames 1 - 10
Considered airborne from frames 8 - 51

ryu-tatsumaki-senpukyaku-light.name: Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku (Light)
notes: Unleash a series of roundhouse kicks while moving forward. A useful attack for knocking opponents away
Considered airborne from frames 5 - 28

ryu-tatsumaki-senpukyaku-medium.name: Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku (Medium)
notes: Unleash a series of roundhouse kicks while moving forward. A useful attack for knocking opponents away
Considered airborne from frames 8 - 41
Values listed for Super Art gauge increases and Parry or Drive Block induced Drive Gauge increases are per hit

ryu-tatsumaki-senpukyaku-heavy.name: Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku (Heavy)
notes: Unleash a series of roundhouse kicks while moving forward. A useful attack for knocking opponents away
Considered airborne from frames 10 - 61
Values listed for Super Art gauge increases and Parry or Drive Block induced Drive Gauge increases are per hit

ryu-tatsumaki-senpukyaku-od.name: Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku (OD)
notes: Unleash a series of roundhouse kicks while moving forward. A useful attack for knocking opponents away

aerial-ryu-tatsumaki-senpukyaku.name: Aerial Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
notes: A Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku performed while airborne. Useful for altering the trajectory of your jump, or for staging a surprise attack from above
aerial-ryu-tatsumaki-senpukyaku.notes_sc: Can be input during forward jump; cannot hit cross-up; cannot hit crouching opponents (unless they extend a vertical hurtbox), and 1st hit can only hit standing opponents on the final 3 airborne frames of jump; puts opponent into limited juggle state; the last 13f of recovery puts Ryu in a crouching state

aerial-ryu-tatsumaki-senpukyaku-od.name: Aerial Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku (OD)
notes: A Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku performed while airborne. Useful for altering the trajectory of your jump, or for staging a surprise attack from above

high-blade-kick-light.name: High Blade Kick (Light)
notes: A thrust kick that quickly closes distance, and can be used to surprise an opponent between their attacks. Strike with the tip of the foot for maximum effectiveness

high-blade-kick-medium.name: High Blade Kick (Medium)
notes: A thrust kick that quickly closes distance, and can be used to surprise an opponent between their attacks. Strike with the tip of the foot for maximum effectiveness

high-blade-kick-heavy.name: High Blade Kick (Heavy)
notes: A thrust kick that quickly closes distance, and can be used to surprise an opponent between their attacks. Strike with the tip of the foot for maximum effectiveness
Juggle state time is extended when attack lands as a Punish Counter

high-blade-kick-od.name: High Blade Kick (OD)
notes: A thrust kick that quickly closes distance, and can be used to surprise an opponent between their attacks. Strike with the tip of the foot for maximum effectiveness

hashogeki-light.name: Hashogeki (Light)
notes: Concentrate ki in the palm and unleash it directly forward. Useful as a follow-up after blocks and combos
Forces a juggle state when the attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter

hashogeki-medium.name: Hashogeki (Medium)
notes: Concentrate ki in the palm and unleash it directly forward. Useful as a follow-up after blocks and combos
Forces a juggle state when the attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter

hashogeki-heavy.name: Hashogeki (Heavy)
notes: Concentrate ki in the palm and unleash it directly forward. Useful as a follow-up after blocks and combos
Produces a crumple stun for 39 frames when the attack lands

hashogeki-od.name: Hashogeki (OD)
notes: Concentrate ki in the palm and unleash it directly forward. Useful as a follow-up after blocks and combos

denjin-charge-hashogeki-od.name: [Denjin Charge] Hashogeki (OD)
notes: Produces a crumple stun for 39 frames when the attack lands

shinku-hadoken.name: Shinku Hadoken
notes: A Hadoken in which the body's ki is concentrated and condensed into your palms before firing. Its swift speed and invulnerability can quickly turn the tables on an opponent
Invincible to strikes and throws from frame 1 to frame 8.
Values do not change when the attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter
The minimum guaranteed damage is 30%

invuln.strike_throw: Invincible to strikes and throws from frame 1 to frame 8.

shinku-hadoken.notes_sc: 5-hit Super projectile; button strength does not affect projectile speed; opponent cannot jump over projectile after Super freeze if within ~1/2 screen; can be preformed even when an OD Hadoken is on screen

denjin-charge-shinku-hadoken.name: [Denjin Charge] Shinku Hadoken
notes: A Hadoken in which the body's ki is concentrated and condensed into your palms before firing. Its swift speed and invulnerability can quickly turn the tables on an opponent
Invincible to strikes and throws from frame 1 to frame 8.
Values do not change when the attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter
The minimum guaranteed damage is 30%

invuln.strike_throw: Invincible to strikes and throws from frame 1 to frame 8.

shin-hashogeki-level-1.name: Shin Hashogeki (Level 1)
notes: A Hashogeki enhanced through spiritual focus. Hold the button to charge the attack and increase its power
Completely invincible from frame 1 to frame 13
Hold down the button to change the properties of this move
Changes to Level 2 version if the button is held for more than 8 frames and then released
Changes to Level 3 version if the button is held for more than 38 frames and then released
Invincible to strikes and throws from frame 2 to frame 8
Values do not change when the attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter
The minimum guaranteed damage is 40%

invuln.full: Completely invincible from frame 1 to frame 13
invuln.strike_throw: Invincible to strikes and throws from frame 2 to frame 8

shin-shoryuken.name: Shin Shoryuken
notes: A powerful Shoryuken unleashed at short range. An overwhelming technique that aims for an opponent's vitals
Completely invincible from frame 1 to frame 16
Values do not change when the attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter
The minimum guaranteed damage is 50%
Note: Can only be canceled into from special moves

invuln.full: Completely invincible from frame 1 to frame 16

shin-shoryuken.notes_sc: More hits connect on block vs. tall standing characters (these extra hits do not deal any additional Drive Damage on block); active frames 3-12 cause a non-cinematic hit that does reduced damage (400x4,2000); cannot anti-air cross-ups; starts up faster only when canceled from HP Shoryuken; cinematic time regenerates ~2.2 Drive bars for Ryu

shin-shoryuken-ca.name: Shin Shoryuken (Critical Art)
notes: When under 25% vitality
A powerful Shoryuken unleashed at short range. An overwhelming technique that aims for an opponent's vitals
Completely invincible from frame 1 to frame 16
Values do not change when the attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter
The minimum guaranteed damage is 50%
Note: Can only be canceled into from special moves

invuln.full: Completely invincible from frame 1 to frame 16

shoulder-throw.name: Shoulder Throw
notes: When near opponent
Grab your opponent and—bearing the brunt of their weight with your arm and back—toss them forward. Throws your opponent without switching places with them
Properties change to the following when the attack lands as a Punish Counter:
- Deals 2040 damage
- Super Art gauge increases by 4000
- Forces a hard knockdown
shoulder-throw.notes_sc: Has access to corner throw loops without walking or dashing (whiff 5LP or manually time the next throw); a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

somersault-throw.name: Somersault Throw
notes: When near opponent
Grab your opponent, place your foot on their torso, and throw them behind you as you fall backwards. Throws your opponent while switching places with them
Properties change to the following when the attack lands as a Punish Counter:
- Deals 2040 damage
- Super Art gauge increases by 4000
- Forces a hard knockdown
somersault-throw.notes_sc: Side switches; no throw/strike oki after back throwing opponent into corner; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

denjin-charge.name: Denjin Charge
notes: Envelop your fists with the Power of Nothingness, enhancing moves such as the Hadoken and Hashogeki. Leaves you wide open, making the timing of its usage critical
Denjin Charge stock added on the 51st frame. Only 1 Denjin Charge stock can be held at a time
Hadoken, Hashogeki, Shinku Hadoken, and Shin Hashogeki’s properties are enhanced when a Denjin Charge is held
denjin-charge.notes_sc: Charges up next fireball-based move (Hadoken, Hashogeki, SA1, SA2) into a more powerful Denjin version; Ryu is in a counter-hit state for the entire animation; gains the Denjin charge on frame 50; can only store 1 stock at a time (does not persist between rounds); can also be performed after 5HP~HK or 5MP~LK~HK (on hit) by inputting 2P
